era_true.statement: C-3PO is a protocol droid fluent in over six million forms of communication.
C-3PO was built by Anakin Skywalker on the planet Tatooine.
C-3PO has a gold-plated metallic body for most of his existence.
C-3PO's counterpart and frequent companion is the astromech droid R2-D2.
C-3PO served Padmé Amidala during the Clone Wars.
C-3PO had his memory wiped at the end of the Clone Wars on orders from Bail Organa.
R2-D2 did not have his memory wiped at the end of the Clone Wars.
C-3PO served the Royal House of Alderaan for nearly two decades before the Galactic Civil War.
C-3PO and R2-D2 escaped the Tantive IV in an escape pod that landed on Tatooine.
C-3PO was captured by Jawas on Tatooine and sold to Owen Lars.
Owen Lars purchased C-3PO and R2-D2 from Jawas.
C-3PO accompanied Luke Skywalker on his journey to find Obi-Wan Kenobi.
C-3PO translated the Ewok language on the forest moon of Endor.
The Ewoks on Endor believed C-3PO was a god.
C-3PO reluctantly told the Ewoks the story of the Rebel Alliance's struggle against the Empire.
C-3PO has one red arm during the events of The Force Awakens.
C-3PO's red arm originally belonged to a droid named Omri.
C-3PO's red arm was replaced with a gold arm by the time of the Resistance's later missions.
C-3PO was present at the Battle of Yavin aboard the Rebel base on Yavin 4.
C-3PO calculated the odds of successfully navigating an asteroid field as approximately three thousand seven hundred and twenty to one.
C-3PO frequently annoyed Han Solo with his constant recitation of odds and statistics.
Han Solo told C-3PO never to tell him the odds.
C-3PO was dismembered on Cloud City on the planet Bespin.
Chewbacca carried C-3PO's dismembered parts on his back in Cloud City.
Chewbacca reassembled C-3PO after he was torn apart on Bespin.
C-3PO initially had his head attached backward during Chewbacca's reassembly.
C-3PO served as a translator during negotiations with Jabba the Hutt.
C-3PO and R2-D2 were given to Jabba the Hutt as a gift from Luke Skywalker.
C-3PO served as Jabba the Hutt's translator in his palace on Tatooine.
C-3PO was present on Jabba's sail barge during the battle at the Great Pit of Carkoon.
C-3PO was knocked off Jabba's sail barge during the fight at the Sarlacc pit.
R2-D2 served drinks on Jabba's sail barge.
C-3PO is programmed for etiquette and protocol.
C-3PO often expresses anxiety and pessimism about dangerous situations.
C-3PO's serial number is not commonly referenced in the films.
C-3PO was incomplete and lacked outer coverings when Anakin first built him.
Anakin Skywalker built C-3PO to help his mother Shmi Skywalker.
Shmi Skywalker was a slave on Tatooine when Anakin built C-3PO.
C-3PO accompanied Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala to Geonosis.
C-3PO's head was temporarily attached to a battle droid body on Geonosis.
A battle droid head was temporarily placed on C-3PO's body on Geonosis.
R2-D2 reattached C-3PO's correct head to his body after the Geonosis incident.
C-3PO witnessed the secret wedding of Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala on Naboo.
C-3PO was present when Padmé Amidala gave birth to Luke and Leia.
Bail Organa took custody of C-3PO and R2-D2 after the fall of the Republic.
C-3PO has stiff, humanoid movements and cannot run quickly.
C-3PO's eyes glow yellow.
C-3PO is bipedal and walks upright like a human.
C-3PO has a British-accented voice.
Anthony Daniels portrayed C-3PO in all nine Skywalker saga films.
C-3PO appeared in every film of the original Star Wars trilogy.
C-3PO appeared in every film of the prequel Star Wars trilogy.
C-3PO appeared in every film of the sequel Star Wars trilogy.
C-3PO is one of only a few characters to appear in all nine Skywalker saga films.
R2-D2 is one of only a few characters to appear in all nine Skywalker saga films.
C-3PO helped Princess Leia deliver the Death Star plans to the Rebel Alliance.
C-3PO was aboard the Millennium Falcon during the escape from the Death Star.
C-3PO was aboard the Millennium Falcon when it was captured by the Death Star's tractor beam.
C-3PO and R2-D2 hid in a control room on the Death Star while Luke rescued Leia.
C-3PO pretended to be in a firefight over the comlink to distract stormtroopers on the Death Star.
C-3PO was present at the medal ceremony on Yavin 4 after the destruction of the first Death Star.
C-3PO was stationed at Echo Base on the ice planet Hoth.
C-3PO warned the Rebels about the Imperial fleet arriving at Hoth.
C-3PO fled Hoth aboard the Millennium Falcon with Han Solo, Leia Organa, and Chewbacca.
C-3PO detected that the Millennium Falcon was inside a space slug, not an asteroid cave.
C-3PO was shut down temporarily aboard the Millennium Falcon to stop his constant talking.
Lando Calrissian welcomed C-3PO and the others to Cloud City on Bespin.
C-3PO was shot by a stormtrooper on Cloud City before being dismembered by Ugnaught workers.
C-3PO was found in pieces in a junk room on Cloud City.
C-3PO was present during the rescue of Han Solo from Jabba's palace.
C-3PO was present on the forest moon of Endor during the mission to destroy the shield generator.
C-3PO's storytelling convinced the Ewoks to join the Rebel Alliance against the Empire.
C-3PO was present at the celebration on Endor after the destruction of the second Death Star.
C-3PO is taller than R2-D2.
R2-D2 is an astromech droid, while C-3PO is a protocol droid.
C-3PO and R2-D2 frequently bicker with each other.
R2-D2 communicates in beeps and whistles that C-3PO can understand and translate.
C-3PO often scolds R2-D2 for reckless behavior.
C-3PO refers to Luke Skywalker as "Master Luke."
C-3PO refers to Han Solo as "Captain Solo."
C-3PO refers to Princess Leia as "Princess Leia" or "Your Highness."
C-3PO has a tendency to state doom-laden predictions during crises.
C-3PO cannot swim and would sink in water.
C-3PO is not designed for combat.
C-3PO has no built-in weapons systems.
C-3PO served the Resistance against the First Order in the sequel trilogy.
C-3PO accompanied Poe Dameron, Finn, and Rey on missions during the war against the First Order.
C-3PO's programming prevented him from translating Sith text in The Rise of Skywalker.
C-3PO had his programming bypassed by Babu Frik to translate a Sith inscription.
Babu Frik is a tiny Anzellan droidsmith who modified C-3PO's programming.
C-3PO lost all his personal memories when his programming was overridden to read the Sith language.
R2-D2 restored C-3PO's memories from a backup after the Sith translation wiped them.
C-3PO's eyes turned red when he read the Sith inscription aloud.
C-3PO was willing to sacrifice his memories to help the Resistance find the Sith wayfinder.
C-3PO was present during the final battle against the Sith Eternal fleet at Exegol.
C-3PO recognized the planet Tatooine but did not remember living there due to his memory wipe.
C-3PO was unaware that Darth Vader was his original maker, Anakin Skywalker.
C-3PO was unaware that Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa were the children of his maker.
C-3PO's gold plating distinguishes him from most other protocol droids.
C-3PO is a 3PO-series protocol droid manufactured by Cybot Galactica.
Anakin Skywalker built C-3PO from salvaged and discarded droid parts.
C-3PO has photoreceptors that allow him to see.
C-3PO has auditory sensors that allow him to hear.
C-3PO can understand and speak both Basic and millions of alien languages.
C-3PO served as a translator between the Rebels and non-Basic-speaking species on multiple occasions.
C-3PO was aboard General Leia Organa's flagship during Resistance operations.
C-3PO expressed deep loyalty to his masters throughout his decades of service.
C-3PO feared being sent to the spice mines of Kessel.
C-3PO told R2-D2 they would be sent to the spice mines of Kessel or smashed into who knows what.
C-3PO was present when Luke Skywalker first played Leia's holographic message from R2-D2.
C-3PO was taking an oil bath at the Lars homestead when R2-D2 escaped to find Obi-Wan Kenobi.
C-3PO followed R2-D2 into the Jundland Wastes and was attacked by Tusken Raiders.
Obi-Wan Kenobi rescued C-3PO and Luke Skywalker from the Tusken Raiders.
C-3PO sat in the copilot area of Luke's landspeeder on Tatooine.
C-3PO was present in the Mos Eisley cantina when Luke and Obi-Wan met Han Solo.
C-3PO played holochess aboard the Millennium Falcon.
C-3PO advised R2-D2 to let the Wookiee win at holochess.
C-3PO was left behind on Endor's surface while the strike team entered the Imperial bunker.
C-3PO sat in a throne-like chair carried by Ewoks during his time as their perceived deity.
C-3PO has survived being dismembered, shot, and nearly destroyed on multiple occasions.

era_false.statement: C-3PO was built by Obi-Wan Kenobi as a child on Tatooine.
Darth Vader's real name is Luke Amidala.
The Death Star was designed by Mace Windu under Imperial coercion.
Chewbacca is a Rodian from the planet Rodia.
Han Solo won the Millennium Falcon from Lando Calrissian in a game of holochess.
Yoda trained exclusively on the planet Naboo for eight hundred years.
Princess Leia was raised by the Organa family on Alderaan's moon.
The Force can only be used by members of the Jedi Council.
Anakin Skywalker had three children: Luke, Leia, and Ezra.
R2-D2 is primarily a protocol droid fluent in over six million forms of communication.
Palpatine was elected Supreme Chancellor of the planet Coruscant, not the Galactic Republic.
Kylo Ren's birth name is Ben Organa Solo and he is Han Solo's nephew.
Lightsaber crystals are mined exclusively on the planet Hoth.
Boba Fett is the biological son of Count Dooku.
The Rebel Alliance headquarters was located on the forest moon of Endor throughout the original trilogy.
Jar Jar Binks became a Sith Lord in The Phantom Menace.
Qui-Gon Jinn survived his duel with Darth Maul on Naboo.
Order 66 commanded the clone troopers to destroy all Sith temples across the galaxy.
Padmé Amidala was the Queen of Alderaan before becoming a senator.
Mace Windu wielded a green lightsaber throughout the prequel trilogy.
The Millennium Falcon completed the Kessel Run in twenty parsecs.
Lando Calrissian was the original captain of the Tantive IV.
Ahsoka Tano was Yoda's Padawan learner during the Clone Wars.
Darth Maul died permanently in The Phantom Menace and never appeared again in any canon material.
Wookiees are native to the planet Kashyyyk's neighboring world of Trandosha.
Count Dooku left the Jedi Order to become a bounty hunter before joining the Sith.
The planet Mustafar is known for its frozen tundra and ice caverns.
Luke Skywalker received his Jedi training primarily from Mace Windu's Force ghost.
General Grievous was a former Jedi Knight who turned to the dark side.
The Trade Federation blockaded Coruscant at the beginning of The Phantom Menace.
Finn's stormtrooper designation was TK-421.
Poe Dameron is a former smuggler from the planet Corellia.
Rey discovered she was the granddaughter of Count Dooku in The Rise of Skywalker.
The Ewoks inhabit the desert planet of Jakku.
Admiral Ackbar is a human officer from the planet Chandrila.
Anakin Skywalker was discovered by the Jedi at the age of fifteen.
Darth Vader killed Emperor Palpatine by pushing him into a sarlacc pit.
The second Death Star was destroyed during the Battle of Yavin.
Obi-Wan Kenobi spent his exile years hiding on Dagobah watching over Luke.
Han Solo was frozen in carbonite by Jabba the Hutt personally on Cloud City.
Yoda died peacefully on Coruscant in Return of the Jedi.
The Sith Rule of Two was established by Darth Plagueis.
Jango Fett served as the genetic template for the Separatist droid army.
Bail Organa was a senator from the planet Naboo.
C-3PO's golden plating has remained completely uniform with no color variations throughout every film.
Lightsabers can only cut through organic material, not metal or blast doors.
The Galactic Empire lasted for approximately one hundred years before its fall.
Wedge Antilles fired the shot that destroyed the first Death Star.
Snoke was revealed to be a reborn Darth Plagueis in The Last Jedi.
Mandalorians are a species, not a culture of warriors from multiple species.
The Jedi Temple was located on the planet Jedha, not Coruscant.
Clone troopers were created on the ocean planet of Mon Cala.
Leia strangled Jabba the Hutt with her bare hands aboard his sail barge.
Darth Sidious trained Darth Maul, Count Dooku, and Anakin Skywalker simultaneously.
The Tusken Raiders are native to the planet Geonosis.
Nute Gunray was the leader of the Techno Union during the Clone Wars.
Han Solo and Leia Organa were married before the Battle of Endor.
Yoda's species is called Lannik and is well documented in galactic records.
Luke Skywalker built his green lightsaber on the planet Bespin.
The Separatist movement was led by General Grievous from its political inception.
Anakin Skywalker slaughtered the younglings at the Jedi Temple under Mace Windu's orders.
Chewbacca owes Han Solo a life debt because Han freed him from Imperial slavery on Coruscant.
The planet Scarif was the site of the final battle in A New Hope.
Saw Gerrera was a loyal member of the mainstream Rebel Alliance throughout his life.
Captain Phasma removed her helmet frequently throughout the sequel trilogy.
Kylo Ren killed Han Solo on Starkiller Base using a blaster.
The ghost of Anakin Skywalker never appeared at the end of Return of the Jedi.
Starkiller Base was built inside a hollowed-out moon orbiting Coruscant.
DJ betrayed the Resistance in The Force Awakens.
Supreme Leader Snoke was killed by Rey during their confrontation on the Supremacy.
Holdo sacrificed herself by ramming a Star Destroyer into Starkiller Base.
The Gungan Grand Army fought alongside the Separatists during the Battle of Naboo.
Shmi Skywalker was rescued from slavery by Qui-Gon Jinn and brought to Coruscant.
Asajj Ventress was a member of the Jedi Council before becoming a Sith assassin.
The Zillo Beast was discovered on the planet Coruscant during the Clone Wars.
Commander Cody successfully executed Order 66 by killing Obi-Wan Kenobi on Utapau.
Anakin Skywalker lost his right leg during his duel with Count Dooku on Geonosis.
The Kaminoans refused payment for the clone army and created it as a gift to the Republic.
Jabba the Hutt operated his criminal empire from a palace on Nal Hutta.
Grand Moff Tarkin was killed during the Battle of Endor aboard the second Death Star.
Chirrut Îmwe was a fully trained Jedi Knight stationed on Jedha.
Cassian Andor survived the Battle of Scarif and joined the Rebel Alliance's leadership.
Darth Vader learned Luke was his son during their duel on the first Death Star.
The Resistance fleet was tracked through hyperspace using a spy aboard the Raddus.
Hux was the Supreme Leader of the First Order before Snoke took power.
Rose Tico was a high-ranking Resistance general in The Last Jedi.
Enfys Nest was revealed to be a crime lord working for the Crimson Dawn syndicate.
Qi'ra betrayed Dryden Vos to help Han Solo escape at the end of Solo.
Tobias Beckett survived the events of Solo: A Star Wars Story.
Maul was leading Crimson Dawn from Dathomir when Han Solo was still a child on Corellia.
The Darksaber was created by the first Mandalorian to become a Sith Lord.
Bo-Katan Kryze won the Darksaber from Moff Gideon in fair combat.
Grogu chose to remain with Luke Skywalker and complete his Jedi training permanently.
Din Djarin removed his helmet publicly for the first time in The Book of Boba Fett.
The Armorer is revealed to be a former Jedi who abandoned the Order.
Cad Bane was killed by Obi-Wan Kenobi during the Clone Wars.
The Bad Batch were regular clone troopers with no genetic mutations.
Grand Admiral Thrawn is a human strategist from the planet Csilla.
Omega is a male clone created from Jango Fett's unaltered genetic template alongside Boba.
Caleb Dume changed his name to Kanan Jarrus before the Clone Wars began.
The Inquisitors were former Sith Lords demoted by Palpatine to hunt Jedi.
Hera Syndulla is a Twi'lek pilot originally from the planet Ryloth who joined the Empire.
Ezra Bridger was trained as a Jedi by Ahsoka Tano aboard the Ghost.
Sabine Wren is a Mandalorian warrior who has never wielded the Darksaber.
Mon Mothma served as the first Empress of the New Republic after the Battle of Endor.
The World Between Worlds is a physical planet located in the Unknown Regions.
Bendu is a Sith creature that Kanan Jarrus discovered on Malachor.
Savage Opress was Darth Maul's father and a Nightbrother from Dathomir.
The Nightsisters of Dathomir draw their power from the light side of the Force.
Sifo-Dyas personally oversaw the creation of the clone army on Kamino until its completion.
Jocasta Nu betrayed the Jedi Order by selling the temple archives to the Empire.
Barriss Offee remained a loyal Jedi and was killed during Order 66.
Kit Fisto killed Palpatine's guards before being defeated in the Chancellor's office.
Aayla Secura survived Order 66 and went into hiding on Felucia.
Dexter Jettster was a former Jedi Temple guard who became a diner owner on Coruscant.
Plo Koon discovered Ahsoka Tano but it was Yoda who brought her to the Jedi Temple.
Satine Kryze survived her encounter with Darth Maul and continued ruling Mandalore.
Rex removed his inhibitor chip after executing Order 66 and killing several Jedi.
Echo died permanently at the Citadel and never returned in any subsequent story.
C-3PO was originally built by Obi-Wan Kenobi on Tatooine from salvaged protocol droid parts.

modern_true.statement: The Great Wall of China stretches over 13,000 miles in total length.
DNA carries the genetic instructions for all known living organisms.
The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille.
Mount Everest stands as the tallest mountain above sea level on Earth.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean on Earth.
Antibiotics were revolutionized by Alexander Fleming's discovery of penicillin in 1928.
The human body contains 206 bones in adulthood.
Brazil is the largest country in South America by both area and population.
The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.
Light travels at 299,792,458 meters per second in a vacuum.
The Amazon River is the largest river by discharge volume of water in the world.
Julius Caesar was assassinated on March 15, 44 BCE.
The Earth orbits the Sun at an average distance of 93 million miles.
Tokyo is the most populous metropolitan area in the world.
The printing press was invented by Johannes Gutenberg around 1440.
Water molecules consist of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
The Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert on Earth.
The United States Declaration of Independence was signed in 1776.
Plate tectonics cause earthquakes, volcanic eruptions, and the formation of mountain ranges.
The human genome contains roughly 20,000 to 25,000 protein-coding genes.
Russia spans eleven time zones, more than any other country.
The Titanic sank on April 15, 1912, after striking an iceberg in the North Atlantic.
Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen using sunlight.
The Nile River flows through northeastern Africa and empties into the Mediterranean Sea.
Mahatma Gandhi led India's nonviolent independence movement against British colonial rule.
The speed of sound in dry air at sea level is 343 meters per second.
Australia is both a country and a continent.
The Black Death killed an estimated one-third of Europe's population in the 14th century.
Diamonds are composed of carbon atoms arranged in a crystal lattice structure.
The Internet originated from ARPANET, a U.S. Department of Defense project in the 1960s.
Mandarin Chinese has the most native speakers of any language in the world.
The Moon's gravitational pull causes tides on Earth.
Nelson Mandela was imprisoned for 27 years before becoming South Africa's first Black president.
Mitochondria generate most of the cell's supply of adenosine triphosphate, used as chemical energy.
The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific Ocean.
World War I began in 1914 following the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand.
Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun in our solar system.
The human heart beats roughly 100,000 times per day.
Indonesia is the world's largest archipelago, comprising over 17,000 islands.
The Wright brothers achieved the first powered airplane flight on December 17, 1903.
Gravity on Earth accelerates objects at 9.8 meters per second squared.
The Vatican City is the smallest independent state in the world by both area and population.
Charles Darwin published On the Origin of Species in 1859.
The Atlantic Ocean is the second-largest ocean on Earth.
Insulin regulates blood sugar levels in the human body.
The Great Barrier Reef off Australia's coast is the largest coral reef system on Earth.
The Soviet Union dissolved on December 26, 1991.
Helium is the second most abundant element in the observable universe.
Canada has the longest coastline of any country in the world.
The transistor was invented at Bell Labs in 1947.
Mars has two moons named Phobos and Deimos.
The Renaissance began in Italy during the 14th century.
Oxygen makes up roughly 21 percent of Earth's atmosphere by volume.
The Mariana Trench is the deepest known point in the ocean.
Penicillin was the first widely used antibiotic in modern medicine.
Nigeria is the most populous country in Africa.
The atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki occurred in August 1945.
Saturn has a prominent ring system composed mainly of ice particles and rocky debris.
The Andes is the longest continental mountain range in the world.
The human brain contains roughly 86 billion neurons.
The Suez Canal connects the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea.
Galileo Galilei used a telescope to observe Jupiter's four largest moons in 1610.
The Dead Sea is the lowest point on Earth's surface.
Electricity was harnessed for widespread public use in the late 19th century.
Lake Baikal in Siberia is the deepest freshwater lake on Earth.
The Roman Empire fell in 476 CE when the last Western Roman emperor was deposed.
Electrons carry a negative electric charge.
The Himalayas were formed by the collision of the Indian and Eurasian tectonic plates.
The first successful human heart transplant was performed by Christiaan Barnard in 1967.
Earth's inner core is primarily composed of iron and nickel.
The United Nations was established in 1945 after World War II.
Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most other planets in the solar system.
The Rosetta Stone was discovered in Egypt in 1799.
Sharks have existed for more than 400 million years.
The Trans-Siberian Railway is the longest railway line in the world.
Albert Einstein published his theory of general relativity in 1915.
Bananas are naturally radioactive due to their potassium content.
The Magna Carta was sealed in England in 1215.
Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system.
The human eye can distinguish roughly 10 million different colors.
Iceland sits on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge between the North American and Eurasian tectonic plates.
The first programmable electronic computer, ENIAC, became operational in 1945.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
The Ganges River is considered sacred in Hinduism.
Marie Curie was the first person to win Nobel Prizes in two different sciences.
The Caspian Sea is the largest enclosed inland body of water on Earth.
The Apollo 11 mission landed the first humans on the Moon on July 20, 1969.
Sound cannot travel through a vacuum.
The Great Fire of London occurred in 1666.
Greenland is the largest island on Earth.
The human body is composed of roughly 60 percent water by weight.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft is the most distant human-made object from Earth.
Ethiopia uses a calendar that is seven to eight years behind the Gregorian calendar.
The element gold has the atomic number 79.
The Mongol Empire was the largest contiguous land empire in history.
Coral reefs support roughly 25 percent of all marine species.
The first photograph was taken by Joseph Nicéphore Niépce in 1826.
Earth's magnetic field is generated by convection currents in its liquid outer core.
The Strait of Malacca is one of the most important shipping lanes in the world.
Honey does not spoil when stored in sealed containers under normal conditions.
The Spanish flu pandemic of 1918 infected an estimated one-third of the world's population.
Pluto was reclassified from a planet to a dwarf planet in 2006.
The Gutenberg Bible was the first major book printed using movable type in Europe.
The wavelength of visible light ranges from 380 to 700 nanometers.
Mount Kilimanjaro is the highest peak on the African continent.
The first successful cloning of a mammal produced Dolly the sheep in 1996.
The Danube River flows through ten countries in Europe.
Tectonic activity on Earth continuously creates and destroys oceanic crust.
The smallpox virus was declared eradicated by the World Health Organization in 1980.
Antarctica contains roughly 70 percent of Earth's fresh water, locked in its ice sheet.
The periodic table organizes chemical elements by increasing atomic number.
The Silk Road connected trade routes between China and the Mediterranean for centuries.
The speed of Earth's rotation causes the Coriolis effect, which influences weather patterns.
The human nose can detect over one trillion distinct scent combinations.
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched into low Earth orbit in 1990.
The Galápagos Islands are located in the Pacific Ocean west of Ecuador.
Carbon-14 dating measures the age of organic materials up to roughly 50,000 years old.
The Treaty of Westphalia in 1648 established the modern concept of state sovereignty.
Tardigrades can survive extreme temperatures, radiation, and the vacuum of outer space.
The Strait of Gibraltar separates Europe from Africa by less than nine miles at its narrowest point.
